basic_info.name: Perovskite Solar Cell Solvent Engineering
basic_info.name_ja: ペロブスカイト太陽電池溶媒工学
basic_info.description: HSP analysis guides selection of processing solvents and anti-solvents for perovskite thin-film deposition, critically affecting film morphology, grain size, crystallinity, and defect density. The HSP distance between the perovskite precursor complex (e.g., PbI2-DMSO or MAI-PbI2-DMF adducts) and the processing solvent determines dissolution quality, while the HSP distance between the anti-solvent and the wet precursor film controls nucleation kinetics and crystal growth. Optimal solvent systems produce uniform, pinhole-free perovskite films with large grain sizes, directly improving power conversion efficiency (PCE) and device stability. HSP-based screening accelerates solvent system discovery, reducing the experimental search space for new perovskite compositions including mixed-halide and all-inorganic variants.

hsp_principle.hsp_principle: Perovskite precursors form Lewis acid-base adducts with coordinating solvents (DMF, DMSO, NMP), and the stability of these adducts correlates with HSP affinity. The processing solvent must have low Ra to the precursor complex to ensure complete dissolution and homogeneous solution. During anti-solvent dripping, a solvent with high Ra to the perovskite but miscibility with the processing solvent is introduced, rapidly reducing precursor solubility and triggering supersaturation-driven nucleation. The HSP difference between processing solvent and anti-solvent (Ra_solvent-antisolvent) influences the rate of solvent exchange and thus nucleation density: moderate Ra produces controlled nucleation with large grains, while very high Ra causes rapid precipitation with small grains. The hydrogen bonding parameter delta_h is particularly important because it governs the donor-acceptor interactions between solvents and Pb2+ centers in the precursor. Solvent blending with calculated HSP enables fine control of intermediate phase formation and crystallization pathway.
hsp_principle.key_equation: Ra = sqrt(4*(dD_solvent - dD_precursor)^2 + (dP_solvent - dP_precursor)^2 + (dH_solvent - dH_precursor)^2); Anti-solvent criterion: Ra(anti-solvent, precursor) >> Ra(processing solvent, precursor); Nucleation rate ~ exp(-Delta_G* / kT) where Delta_G* depends on supersaturation controlled by Ra(anti-solvent, processing solvent)
hsp_principle.typical_red_threshold: Processing solvent: RED < 1.0 to precursor; Anti-solvent: RED > 2.0 to precursor; Ra(processing solvent, anti-solvent) in range 5-15 MPa^0.5 for controlled crystallization [uncertain]

required_inputs: HSP of perovskite precursor complex (delta_d, delta_p, delta_h)
Boiling point and vapor pressure of solvents
data_sources: HSPiP database for common organic solvents
Published perovskite precursor HSP from dissolution testing
Group contribution estimation for novel solvent HSP
Literature HSP for DMF, DMSO, GBL, NMP, and common anti-solvents
Experimental solubility sphere determination via systematic dissolution tests
complementary_parameters: Solvent boiling point and evaporation rate

accuracy.limitations: HSP analysis captures thermodynamic solvent-precursor affinity but does not fully describe the complex crystallization kinetics of perovskite formation, including intermediate phase transitions (e.g., delta to alpha phase). Perovskite precursors are ionic/coordination compounds whose interactions extend beyond van der Waals, polar, and hydrogen bonding forces captured by HSP. The strong Lewis acid-base coordination between Pb2+ and donor solvents is only partially reflected in delta_h. HSP of perovskite precursor complexes are composition-dependent and not well-tabulated, requiring experimental determination for each new formulation. Film morphology depends on many non-HSP factors including spin speed, substrate wetting, anti-solvent drip timing, and ambient conditions. Degradation pathways (moisture, heat, light) are not predicted by HSP.
known_failure_modes: Precursor complexes with strong specific coordination (e.g., DMSO-PbI2 adducts) where Lewis acid-base interactions dominate over general HSP affinity
Mixed-cation perovskites (FA/MA/Cs) where multiple precursor species have different HSP
Anti-solvent volume and timing effects that override thermodynamic HSP predictions
High-humidity environments where water ingress during processing alters crystallization regardless of solvent HSP
Scalable deposition methods (slot-die, blade coating) where fluid dynamics matter more than equilibrium solubility
accuracy.validation_method: SEM and AFM imaging of perovskite film morphology (grain size, coverage, pinhole density), XRD for crystallinity and phase purity, UV-Vis absorption and photoluminescence for optical quality, solar cell J-V characterization for PCE, stability testing under ISOS protocols

key_references.title: Solvent engineering for high-performance inorganic-organic hybrid perovskite solar cells

literature.regulatory_relevance: Lead-containing perovskites fall under RoHS and REACH restrictions on hazardous substances. HSP-guided green solvent substitution supports compliance with EU Directive 2011/65/EU (RoHS) and worker safety regulations by replacing toxic solvents (DMF, NMP) with safer alternatives. No regulatory body currently mandates HSP analysis, but it aids in meeting VOC emission standards and workplace exposure limits.

industry_sectors: Flexible electronics and wearable energy harvesting
commercial_tools: Scienomics MAPS (molecular simulation for perovskite systems)
GreatCell Solar / Solaronix (perovskite materials suppliers with formulation guides)
